label: Formal verification, cbmc 6.9.0 (C bus ring, exhaustive)

environment.caveat: exhaustive over ALL inputs (not sampled). Atomics are modelled as their sequential equivalent for these single-threaded proofs (cbmc_atomic_shim.h); concurrent interleavings are covered by the SymbiYosys/k-induction RTL proof.

results.name: PROP_safety
results.note: ring_put/ring_get never index out of bounds or deref a bad pointer, for ANY 64-bit position

results.name: PROP_roundtrip
results.note: put(w) then get(w) returns EXACTLY the stamp+payload written, and reports success

results.name: PROP_reject_writing
results.note: a reader meeting a slot mid-write (odd sequence) rejects it, no torn read

results.name: PROP_reject_stale
results.note: a reader meeting a slot holding a different published generation rejects it

results.name: PROP_backpressure
results.note: within the producer's backpressure window (< ring size) no two live positions alias a slot

note: Machine-checked proofs of bench/native/ring.h, the SAME ring nsbus.c benchmarks. SUCCESS means cbmc proved no counterexample exists up to the bound.
